34 CFR § 99.31(a)(1)(i)(B).rationale: Statute covers outsourced school officials generally, but regulation adds direct-control requirement central to these facts and answer.
Provides the outsourced-party school-official rule, direct-control, and redisclosure conditions not supplied by any statute here.

34 CFR § 99.31(a)(1)(i)(A).rationale: Supplies the 'legitimate educational interest' standard used to limit sharing to records needed for the team's professional responsibility.

34 CFR § 99.12(a).rationale: Statute gives general inspect/review right; §99.12(a) supplies the multi-student record rule limiting access to own child's info or being informed of it.

34 CFR § 99.3.rationale: Statute already defines education records as maintained records. Answer turns on personal observation not yet recorded; cited reg exclusion about post-student records is irrelevant.

34 CFR § 99.7(a)(1).rationale: Statute requires informing parents, but this regulation adds the key annual timing requirement the answer evaluates.

34 CFR § 99.7(b).rationale: Provides the operative rule that notice may be by any means reasonably likely to inform; decisive to concluding no mailing was required.

34 CFR § 99.21(a).rationale: It restates the statute's hearing right for inaccurate/misleading/privacy-violating records. The core answer that no grade change is required follows from 20 U.S.C. § 1232g(a)(2).

34 CFR § 99.5(a)(1).rationale: It only confirms rights transferred to the student while alive. The answer turns on guidance that FERPA rights lapse at death, not on this regulation.

34 CFR § 99.31(a)(1)(i)(B).rationale: Supplies the school-official outsourcing conditions: institutional service, direct control, and FERPA use limits. No statute source here provides those specifics.

34 CFR § 99.33(a)(1).rationale: Provides the operative no-redisclosure/use limitation incorporated by §99.31(a)(1)(i)(B)(3), supporting why vendor data use beyond the service defeats the exception.

34 CFR § 99.31(a)(9)(ii)(A).rationale: Statute gives a general advance-notice rule for subpoenas; this regulation supplies the key exception for federal grand jury subpoenas with a nondisclosure order.

34 CFR § 99.5(a)(1).rationale: Restates 20 U.S.C. § 1232g(d): rights transfer to the student upon postsecondary attendance or age 18. Statute alone supports this point.

34 CFR § 99.5(b).rationale: Only says FERPA does not prevent disclosure to a parent if a §99.31 exception applies. The decisive dependent-student rule is elsewhere, not in this cited regulation.
